ocr.text: The table presents an analysis of bile salts across various animals, detailing specific bile salts associated with each species. The
header "Animal" indicates the type of animal, while the header "Bile salts" specifies the corresponding bile salts. The bear is
associated with bile salts TUDC, TC, TDC, and TCDC. In contrast, the dog possesses bile salts TC, TCDC, and TDC. The cattle
exhibit a wider variety, including TC, TDC, TCDC, TLC, GC, GDC, GCDC, GLC, and AC.
Animal  Bile salts
Bear  TUDC, TC, TDC, TCDC
Dog  TC, TCDC, TDC
Cattle  TC, TDC, TCDC, TLC, GC, GDC, GCDC, GLC, AC
Pig  TC, TDC, TCDC, THC, THDC, GC, GDC, GCDC, GHC, GHDC, GHOC
Snake  TPC, TC, TDC
Rabbit  GDC, TC, TDC, TCDC
Chicken  TC, TCDC, TAC
Fish  TCDC, TC, TDC, AC
Goose  TCDC, TPHC, TBC
Duck  TCDC, TPHC, TBC
Mouse  TC, TDC, TCDC, T-β-MC, T-ω-MC, TUDC
The pig has a diverse range as well, with TC, TDC, TCDC, THC, THDC, GC, GDC, GCDC, GHC, GHDC, and GHOC. The
snake is noted for having bile salts TPC, TC, and TDC, while the rabbit includes GDC, TC, TDC, and TCDC. The chicken is
identified with TC, TCDC, and TAC, whereas the fish has TCDC, TC, TDC, and AC. The goose contains TCDC, TPHC, and
TBC, and similarly, the duck features TCDC, TPHC, and TBC. Finally, the mouse is detailed with bile salts TC, TDC, TCDC,
T-β-MC, T-ω-MC, and TUDC. Each row reflects the unique combination of bile salts present in these animals, emphasizing the
diversity in bile salt composition across species, with the cattle and pig showcasing the most extensive variety. The information
underscores the biochemical complexity and evolutionary adaptations of these animals concerning their digestive processes.
